title: Reevaluating the PWM Monster
wikitext:
[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Thu Apr 14, 2011 5:57 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Hehe, yeah. While that would be interesting, I don't really see it working. I guess maybe technically speaking, Adam and Lilith sort of come from the same flesh (being PWM) and due to having souls of the FAR--as well as carrying some FAR souls with them as they journeyed to Earth. But Kaworu and Rei being of the same flesh (besides Lilin, I mean) I can't really see. Hopefully that made sense with how I articulated it, I'm feeling rather high-strung today.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Apr 14, 2011 6:31 pm Yeah... I used to be a huge PWM advocate, but I think it's gotten out of hand. We've liberally throw about this term that's based on barely anything, while practically ignoring the stuff about patterns/blood types that is mentioned throughout the show. And even after I confirm that the replica Spears are made of LCL, and deduce that LCL probably comes from 'another universe' (which makes LCL itself some pretty special shit), the PWM machine continues to grind on, totally oblivious. I've been calling for a total overhaul of how we approach this stuff for a while now, though maybe it wouldn't helped if I'd attempted to focus efforts with a thread or something... FWIW, I think that the tidbit about Shamshel is intended to apply to the Angels in general -- kind of like how Sandalphon implies that all Angels start out as humanoids in egg cases.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Thu Apr 14, 2011 6:43 pm Reichu wrote: And even after I confirm that the replica Spears are made of LCL, and deduce that LCL probably comes from 'another universe' (which makes LCL itself some pretty special shit), the PWM machine continues to grind on, totally oblivious.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Apr 14, 2011 6:44 pm I thought particle wave matter didn't just rely on Ritsuko's Episode 05 talk (Ritsuko: "We do know the Angel is composed of a type of matter characterized by both particulate and wave properties, like light.") , but also her words in Episode 23? (Ritsuko: "Yes, this is where she was born. The water and light that form Rei's subconscious must have a strong impression of this place.") Of course, two lines of dialogue does not complete information make. It's fanon that probably should be "reevaluated", yeah. Edit: Ninja'ed by Monk Ed.

[]:             Quote Post by Fireand'chutes77 » Fri Apr 15, 2011 1:25 am Monk Ed wrote: The fact that the replica Spears were confirmed to be made of LCL absolutely sealed it for me...

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Fri Apr 15, 2011 2:01 am Why in the world would Ritsuko be talking about what Rei's body is made of when she's talking about her "subconscious"? And why would talking about it being made of light and water be equal to saying that it's particle-wave matter? Anyway, to get this newly splitted-out topic started: The thesis I have been pimping (quietly) for two years now is that contrary to popular belief, a soul does not have to be present to keep a body from reverting to LCL. Instead, I posit that all flesh, even that of a Lilin, must be actively broken down (such as by an AATF or "destrudo") in order to cause it to revert to LCL. Look at the similarities between all mentions of destrudo in the show. There are three that I can recall offhand: The first is when they try to recover Shinji from the entry plug; the second is when the Rei clones are destroyed by a destrudo release; the third is when Shinji is in the cockpit in EoE, prior to P3II. All of these mentions of destrudo are in the context of dissolving the physical form -- the "ego", in Eva metaphor-lingo. And in the case of the Rei clones, this includes physical forms with no soul attached to begin with . The same thing which is used to dissolve the ego border of a living pilot is also used to dissolve a soulless body. This would imply that the soulless body, too, is an ego border -- meaning that the ego border (the body) can exist even without a soul inside. If this is the case, it means we need not attribute any special shape-holding powers to PWM. Thankfully, it also means we no longer have to explain why toenail clippings don't tang. (I would also point out how Gendo's half-tanged remains remained even after his soul had been removed, but I already know that people will discount that scene because of all the wacky shit that was going on anyway.)

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Fri Apr 15, 2011 5:15 am Wasn't the reason we assumed that Lilith was made from PWM was because Kaworu was, and Kaworu and Rei were the same, and Rei II+ was grown/cloned from Lilith's body? I think most people took that, combined it with the fact that we KNOW PWM can radically change shape when combined with a soul, or even perhaps change shape with the aid of an S2, and ran with it. Lilim seem incapable of doing anything except hanging together and dissolving to LCL and disembodied souls during 3I. I think I could agree with Monk Ed that flesh/LCL doesn't necessarily need a soul to hold it together, and that the reversion to LCL could be a particular element of destrudo, but I don't think this helps us answer a lot of other questions, like why all "Lilim" are the same but the Angels radically different, why Lilith changes into GNR in EoE, etc.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Fri Apr 15, 2011 5:39 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: Wasn't the reason we assumed that Lilith was made from PWM was because Kaworu was, and Kaworu and Rei were the same, and Rei II+ was grown/cloned from Lilith's body? I think most people took that, combined it with the fact that we KNOW PWM can radically change shape when combined with a soul, or even perhaps change shape with the aid of an S2, and ran with it. Lilim seem incapable of doing anything except hanging together and dissolving to LCL and disembodied souls during 3I. I think I could agree with Monk Ed that flesh/LCL doesn't necessarily need a soul to hold it together, and that the reversion to LCL could be a particular element of destrudo, but I don't think this helps us answer a lot of other questions, like why all "Lilim" are the same but the Angels radically different, why Lilith changes into GNR in EoE, etc.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Apr 15, 2011 9:32 am Bit of an aside here... Fireand'chutes77 wrote: I found her "water and light" line an unexpectedly beautiful bit of wordplay, and didn't really consider she might be talking about a different kind of matter.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Apr 15, 2011 10:00 am Monk Ed wrote: Nerv learned how to identify Angel ATFs by studying Shamshel's corpse .

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Fri Apr 15, 2011 3:14 pm Reichu wrote: Wellllll....

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Apr 15, 2011 3:22 pm I don't think it was the Type Blue that was the discovery so much as "...Hey, these things are basically hard light, what the fuck?"

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Fri Apr 15, 2011 3:31 pm AuraTwilight wrote: I don't think it was the Type Blue that was the discovery so much as "...Hey, these things are basically hard light, what the fuck?"

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Apr 15, 2011 4:10 pm What she shows them appears to be two sets of 24 chromosomes. SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Fri Apr 15, 2011 4:23 pm How can a pattern with the wrong number of chromosomes be 99.89% similar to human DNA? Or are they only counting the parts that, uh, overlap?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Apr 15, 2011 4:25 pm I dunno. Maybe they're cheating by only counting one sex chromosome from each set.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sat Apr 16, 2011 2:56 am Monk Ed wrote: Under this new framework, whether Rei is PWM or not is no longer a concern, because the PWM is not what matters anymore; if we theorize that ATF somehow "bonds" to flesh, that is sufficient to explain why the characters have the powers that they do.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Apr 16, 2011 5:52 pm So apparently the Angels are all herms.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Sat Apr 16, 2011 6:43 pm Hhh hot . Eva Yojimbo wrote: I read this post carefully, but I don't think it explains everything or, perhaps, I'm missing something. To clarify; are you suggesting that the ATF is only factor when it comes to shaping form, no matter whether it's LCL/flesh or PWM?

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sat Apr 16, 2011 9:06 pm Reichu wrote: And the "extra" chromosome is an additional sex chromosome, for a total of four across both sets (YX, YX). Whoa. So apparently the Angels are all herms. And I just checked the graphic from when GNR is getting scanned in 26', and it's the same one... so GNR is also a herm.
